claim.observation: windows.netscan shows local sockets on this host bound to two distinct private IPv4 addresses: 10.10.6.15 (e.g. ncpa_listener. LISTENING on 10.10.6.15:5693; java.exe ESTABLISHED 10.10.6.15:49203 -> 10.10.200.207:5672) and 172.16.6.15 (e.g. System NetBIOS UDP on 172.16.6.15:137 and :138; and 172.16.6.15:3262 ESTABLISHED to 172.16.5.50). svchost.exe PID 392 is LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3389 (TCPv4 and TCPv6) and System (PID 4) is LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:445.
claim.interpretation: The host is dual-homed, with interfaces in both the 10.10.6.0 and 172.16.6.0 segments, and exposes the RDP service (TCP 3389) and SMB (TCP 445). A machine straddling two subnets that also serves RDP/SMB is well positioned to bridge the segments and act as a lateral-movement pivot. No ESTABLISHED inbound session on 3389 appears in this scan, so an active interactive RDP session at the moment of capture is NOT evidenced by netscan alone.
